text: The patient is positioned in the right lateral decubitus position for the robotic left adrenalectomy. A 12mm camera trocar is inserted in the left midclavicular line, 10cm above the umbilicus. An 8mm left arm trocar is placed 7cm above the camera trocar, while the right arm trocar is positioned 7cm below and slightly lateral to the camera trocar. The fourth robotic arm is inserted 5cm below and 4cm lateral to the right arm. A 12mm assistant port is placed in the midline above the umbilicus.

The surgeon begins the procedure by reflecting the descending colon medially. As the dissection progresses towards the spleen, the peritoneum lateral to the spleen is incised in a cephalad direction, allowing for superior medial reflection of the spleen. The fourth robotic arm is used to apply gentle superior medial pressure on the spleen, facilitating the development of the plane between the superior medial aspect of the kidney and the spleen.

The robotic fourth arm applies anterior pressure on the lower pole of the left kidney, facilitating the identification of the left gonadal vein. The surgeon then dissects cephalad towards the left renal vein, revealing the anatomical pathway.

The surgeon divides the tissue medial to the left renal vein to enhance exposure. Following the dissection, the left adrenal vein is identified on the cephalad aspect of the left renal vein. The vein is ligated with two Hem-o-lok clips and subsequently divided.

Following the division of the adrenal vein, the surgeon divides the tissue inferior and medial to the adrenal gland. The fourth robotic arm is utilized to retract the adrenal gland, aiding in its final extirpation.
The surgeon places the adrenalectomy specimen into a retrieval bag for removal from the surgical field.
